# gcloud CLI Safe-Allowlist Policy

**Source enumeration**: `gcloud-cli-commands.json` (gcloud 564.0.0, generated by `scripts/gcloud-allowlist-audit.sh`).
**Audits**: [`gcloud-audit-data-plane.md`](./gcloud-audit-data-plane.md) (data-plane coverage) and [`gcloud-audit-interactive-credential-installs.md`](./gcloud-audit-interactive-credential-installs.md) (interactive / auth / installs-code coverage). Verb-pattern sanity was audited directly in the primary session using the two reports above plus the enumeration.

## Shape of the policy

gcloud's 18,728-command surface rules out per-leaf enumeration. Instead, the policy is **verb-pattern based**: universal `"gcloud * <verb> *"` allows for every verb that reads admin/metadata (list, describe, get-iam-policy, etc.), with layered overrides for data-plane commands, interactive/credential/installs-code hazards, and a blanket ask for `gcloud alpha *` and `gcloud beta *`.

The ordering in `opencode.jsonc` (last-match-wins) is:

1. **Universal SAFE verb allows** — one `"gcloud * <verb>"` + `"gcloud * <verb> *"` pair per verb.
2. **Singleton utility allows** — `info`, `version`, `help`, `topic`, `cheat-sheet`, `auth list`, `config list`, `config configurations list|describe`.
3. **Data-plane asks** — service/resource-specific asks that override tier 1/2 where a SAFE verb would otherwise allow a data-reading or data-writing command (e.g., `gcloud storage objects list` is data, not metadata).
4. **Interactive / session-blocking asks** — `ssh`, `scp`, `start-iap-tunnel`, `connect`, `* operations wait*`, `* wait`, `* stream-logs*`, `emulators * start*`, `init`, `docker`, `feedback`, `survey`.
5. **Auth / credential asks** — login/logout/revoke/print-access-token, the broad `gcloud * get-credentials*` wildcard (covers GKE and all Anthos/Fleet/Hub variants), SA-key create/upload/sign, `sign-url`, token prints, developer-connect tokens.
6. **Components (installs-code) asks** — `install|update|remove|reinstall|repositories *`.
7. **Per-service demotion** — `gcloud artifacts print-settings*` asks even though `print-settings` is a universal SAFE verb, because `--json-key` can embed SA key material in the printed snippet.
8. **Alpha / beta blanket ask** — `gcloud alpha`, `gcloud alpha *`, `gcloud beta`, `gcloud beta *`. Placed **last** in the gcloud section so these asks win over every prior allow.

Total: **39 allow patterns, 210 ask patterns** (249 gcloud-prefixed rules) covering the 5,302 stable leaves plus 9,896 alpha/beta leaves on gcloud 564.0.0.

## Rubric

| Class | Policy | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **SAFE — admin/metadata read** | allow | `list`, `describe`, `get-iam-policy`, `get-ancestors-iam-policy`, `test-iam-permissions`, `get-value`, `get-public-key`, `search`, `lookup`, `explain`, `print-settings`. Plus singleton `info`, `version`, `help`, `topic`, `auth list`, `config list`. |
| **DATA-READ** | ask | `storage ls|cat|du|hash|rsync`, `storage objects describe|list`, `secrets versions access`, `logging read|tail`, `pubsub subscriptions pull`, `kms decrypt|mac-verify|raw-decrypt|asymmetric-decrypt`, `spanner databases execute-sql`, `sql instances execute-sql`, `ai endpoints predict|explain|stream-*`, `functions logs read`, `app logs read|tail`, `run * logs read`, `compute instances get-serial-port-output|get-screenshot`, `asset export|query|get-history`, `infra-manager * export-statefile`, `artifacts * download`, `vector-search collections data-objects describe|list|query|search|batch-search` (+ `export-data-objects`). |
| **DATA-WRITE** | ask | `storage cp|mv|rm|restore|sign-url`, `storage objects *` (write verbs), `storage batch-operations *`, `logging write|copy`, `pubsub topics publish`, `pubsub subscriptions ack`, `kms encrypt|raw-encrypt|asymmetric-encrypt|asymmetric-sign|mac-sign|keys versions import|decapsulate`, `spanner rows insert|update|delete`, `sql export|import`, `alloydb clusters export|import`, `firestore export|import|bulk-delete`, `datastore export|import`, `functions call`, `compute images export|import`, `compute machine-images import`, `compute instances export|import`, `compute routers download-route-policy|upload-route-policy`, `composer environments storage *`, `backup-dr backups restore`, `bigtable backups copy`, `bigtable tables restore`, `redis instances export|import`, `memorystore * export`, `lustre instances export-data|import-data`, `netapp kms-configs encrypt|verify`, `netapp volumes restore-backup-files`, `healthcare * export|import|deidentify`, `vector-search collections data-objects create|update|delete|batch-*`. |
| **METADATA-ADMIN WRITE** | ask (fall-through to `*: ask`) | `create`, `delete`, `update`, `patch`, `set-*`, `add-*`, `remove-*`, `enable`, `disable`, `cancel`, `run`, `submit`, `deploy`, `start`, `stop`, `resume`, `restore`, `rollback`, `revoke`, `reset`, `move`, `rename`, `attach`, `detach`, `bind`, `unbind`, `link`, `unlink`, `connect`, `disconnect`, `migrate`. Not allowed by any universal pattern; no ask pattern is needed — they fall through to the top-level `*: ask` default. |
| **INTERACTIVE** | ask | `compute ssh|scp|start-iap-tunnel|connect-to-serial-port|config-ssh|copy-files|reset-windows-password|instances tail-serial-port-output`, `compute tpus * ssh|scp`, `compute instance-groups managed wait-until*`, `cloud-shell ssh|scp|get-mount-command`, `app instances ssh|scp`, `app logs tail`, `app open-console`, `workstations ssh|start-tcp-tunnel`, `sql connect`, `emulators * start*`, `docker *`, `init`, `feedback`, `survey`, `* operations wait*`, `* wait`, `* stream-logs*`, `dataproc batches wait|jobs wait`, `workflows executions wait|wait-last`. |
| **AUTH / CREDENTIAL** | ask | `auth login|logout|revoke|activate-service-account|print-access-token|print-identity-token|application-default *|configure-docker|enterprise-certificate-config *`, `anthos auth login|create-login-config`, `* get-credentials*` (GKE + Fleet/Hub/Anthos/Attached/AWS/Azure/bare-metal/edge-cloud variants), `iam service-accounts keys create|upload|sign-blob|sign-jwt`, `iam workforce-pools create-login-config|providers keys create`, `iam workload-identity-pools providers keys create`, `compute sign-url`, `storage sign-url`, `redis instances get-auth-string`, `sql generate-login-token`, `developer-connect * fetch-read-token|fetch-read-write-token`, `iap oauth-clients reset-secret`. |
| **INSTALLS-CODE** | ask | `components install|update|remove|reinstall`, `components repositories *`. |
| **ALPHA / BETA** | ask (blanket) | Every leaf under `gcloud alpha` and `gcloud beta`, regardless of verb. |

## Counter-example: `gcloud artifacts print-settings`

`print-settings` is a universal SAFE verb because on most services it just prints a config snippet. On `gcloud artifacts`, the `--json-key=<path>` flag embeds the contents of a service-account key file into the printed output, leaking credential material. This single service therefore has an explicit per-service demotion (`"gcloud artifacts print-settings*": "ask"`) placed after the universal `print-settings` allow.

## What falls through vs. what is explicitly demoted

Most mutating verbs (create, delete, update, enable, disable, …) are NOT in any allow pattern and fall through to the top-level `*: ask`. They are not listed in the ask layer because:

- Explicitly listing them would inflate the ruleset by thousands of rules for no behavioral change.
- The only reason to explicitly demote a verb to ask is if a broader rule might allow it by mistake. The universal SAFE verbs (list, describe, get-*) never match a mutating verb, so mutations remain asked by the top-level default.

Data-plane commands that happen to be named with a SAFE verb (e.g., `gcloud storage objects list`, `gcloud vector-search collections data-objects list`, `gcloud secrets versions access` with a non-SAFE verb name already) require explicit demotion. Hence the tier-3 asks.

Interactive and credential commands ending in SAFE verbs (`get-credentials`, `get-mount-command`, `get-auth-string`, `get-serial-port-output`) are not universally in the SAFE list (`get` is, but these use compound verb names), so the only reason to demote them is to defend against future broadening. The explicit per-command asks serve that purpose.

Alpha / beta would match `"gcloud * list *": "allow"` for reads; the blanket tier-8 ask overrides it.

## Re-run cadence

Re-run `scripts/gcloud-allowlist-audit.sh` whenever the Cloud SDK is bumped (e.g., via `mise` or `brew upgrade google-cloud-sdk`). Diff `docs/research/gcloud-cli-commands.json` and re-classify any new services or new data-plane subcommands. Update the policy and simulator test cases accordingly.

## Validation

`scripts/gcloud-allowlist-simulator.py` ports `Wildcard.match` + `findLast` and runs **169 test cases** covering every tier, the counter-example, and regressions against the gh and top-level allowlist entries. Must pass 100% before any change to the gcloud block lands. The gh simulator (`gh-allowlist-simulator.py`) is preserved and still passes 137/137 tests after the gcloud block is added.

## Coverage summary (gcloud 564.0.0)

| Category | Commands affected | Rule count |
|---|---|---|
| Tier 1 — universal SAFE verb allows | ~1,835 stable leaves | 22 patterns |
| Tier 2 — singleton utility allows | 13 commands | 17 patterns |
| Tier 3 — data-plane asks | ~110 leaves across 33 services | ~100 patterns |
| Tier 4 — interactive asks | ~70 leaves | ~35 patterns |
| Tier 5 — auth / credential asks | ~40 leaves | ~30 patterns |
| Tier 6 — components asks | 6 leaves | 5 patterns |
| Tier 7 — per-service demotion | 1 service | 1 pattern |
| Tier 8 — alpha / beta blanket | 9,896 alpha/beta leaves | 4 patterns |
| Fall-through `*: ask` | remaining ~3,400 mutating stable leaves | 0 new patterns |

Total new gcloud rules: **249** (39 allow / 210 ask).
